text: The surgeon uses a peri-umbilical optical trocar technique to access the abdomen, followed by a laparoscopically guided transversus abdominis plane block. Cannulas are placed in pre-planned positions, and the patient is positioned in twelve degrees of reverse Trendelenburg. Arm one holds bipolar forceps, arm three holds hook cautery, and arm four holds a prograsp, with the camera in arm two. The gallbladder is retracted cephalad, revealing adhesions to the omentum, which are carefully dissected using monopolar cautery while monitoring the duodenum's position to prevent injury.

The surgeon retracts the gallbladder cephalad and uses a hook cautery to incise the peritoneum, developing a dissection plane between the gallbladder and liver. The gallbladder is grasped in a caudal direction, and the peritoneum is retracted cephalad, allowing for a lateral to medial dissection. Indocyanine green fluorescence imaging is utilized to visualize the gallbladder, ensuring a bloodless plane and preserving the integrity of the liver and gallbladder.

The surgeon continues the dissection of the avascular plane along the neck of the gallbladder, carefully avoiding the duodenum while removing adhesions. A lateral to medial dissection is performed to achieve the critical view of safety. The cystic artery and cystic duct are identified using ICG fluorescence. A window is created between these structures, and they are dissected for clear identification. The cystic artery is clipped twice and the cystic duct three times, with an additional clip on the gallbladder side, before being transected. The cystic artery is then transected with electrocautery. The gallbladder is removed from the liver using hook electrocautery, maintaining a bloodless avascular plane.

The surgeon inserts an endocatch bag percutaneously and deposits the completely removed gallbladder into it. The gallbladder is extracted from the abdomen intact, without any spillage of biliary contents. The extraction site is closed using a transvascular suture passer in a figure of eight fashion, showcasing the cosmetic outcome.
